summary: Focused auth-only re-test after E1 swapped phone+OTP for phone+password (JWT bearer). 14/14 backend pytest cases pass covering register (happy/dup/short-pw/short-phone), login (wrong-pw/no-account/correct pre-setup), setup-shop, /auth/me (401/garbage/pre-setup 404/post-setup 200), phone normalization to +91, and password_hash exclusion from every response. Frontend Playwright E2E on mobile viewport (390x844) via the public preview URL: /login loads, Sign-in with unknown phone shows error toast, Sign-up with fresh phone routes to /setup, filling shop/owner and clicking finish-setup routes to /(tabs) dashboard (TEST_UI_Shop rendered), and re-login after clearing localStorage skips /setup and lands straight on the dashboard. No auth defects found.

issues: Deprecation warnings: 'shadow*' style props (use boxShadow) and props.pointerEvents (use style.pointerEvents). Cosmetic only.

action_items: Old test file /app/backend/tests/test_darubahi_api.py referenced defunct /auth/send-otp and /auth/verify-otp endpoints; it has been removed and replaced by test_auth_phone_password.py. If any other feature testing is needed later (products/sales/reports), a new regression suite must be written since the OTP fixtures no longer work.

Minor test-selector nit: data-testid `shop-type-Composite (FL+BB)` contains parentheses/spaces which Playwright can locate but user-agents may sanitize; consider slug testIDs like `shop-type-composite-flbb`. Not a bug — first chip is preselected so signup still completes.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py normalizes phone via lstrip('0') then prefixes +91 only for 10-digit — safe against leading zero and '+91' inputs; unit test test_phone_normalized_to_plus91 confirms parity.

register() overwrites (upserts) an existing user only if password_hash is missing — protects against silent password replacement while still allowing a previously-abandoned +91 record (rare edge from earlier OTP flow) to be claimed. Good.
current_user() correctly returns 404 (not 401) when token is valid but shop not set up — the frontend uses this to trigger /setup routing.
All /auth responses use projection {'_id': 0, 'password_hash': 0} — verified via regex on raw response text in tests; no leak.
JWT expiry 60 days is long — acceptable for a small-shop mobile app but call out in prod hardening notes.

CORSMiddleware uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True — technically invalid per CORS spec (credentials require explicit origins), but this app uses Bearer tokens (not cookies) so it works. Consider tightening later.
test_credentials: Tests generate fresh 10-digit phones per run (9<epoch-ms>) with password 'test1234'. No hardcoded accounts required. Documented seed phones 9876543210 / 9876543211 in /app/memory/test_credentials.md still exist in Mongo from prior runs; new tests avoid them to prevent 'already exists' collisions.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates ~6 users under +91<random 10-digit> in Mongo db=darubahi collection=users, one of which becomes a TEST_UI_Shop / TEST_Shop record. Prefixed with TEST_ for easy cleanup. No cross-test data mutation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth is now phone+password with JWT bearer (Authorization: Bearer <token>) — NO OTP endpoints exist anymore. If you extend testing to products/sales/reports, use the fixture pattern in test_auth_phone_password.py: register a fresh phone → setup-shop → use returned Bearer token. Frontend uses AsyncStorage on native and localStorage on web; token key persists between reloads so clear it via localStorage before testing 'fresh login' scenarios.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no defects found. All 13 spec scenarios from the review request verified.
